title: Error Recovery & Retry Patterns
summary: Framework and library patterns for retry strategies, circuit breakers, offline queuing, and HTTP error classification. Covers TanStack Query, axios-retry, urql retryExchange, SWR, and Apollo RetryLink — analyzing their retry configuration models, backoff algorithms, and offline handling. Grounds the Web Reliability standard's design decisions: why first-that-accepts over a chain object, why null-means-decline, and which patterns are HTTP-specific vs. universally applicable. Extended (2026-06-19) for decision #1032 — the protocol-level error-recovery seam — with the two angles the single-handler JS survey missed: resilience COMPOSITION (Polly PolicyWrap + resilience4j decorators, where multi-concern cooperation is an explicit author-ordered wrap and the order itself is load-bearing — breaker-wraps-retry vs retry-wraps-breaker — so a baked protocol order is wrong; supports a composite-handler answer over protocol orchestration), and recovery-state SURFACING (TanStack's status-vs-fetchStatus split and the circuit-breaker closed/open/half-open canon, establishing a recovery-phase vocabulary normalized at the library, distinct from the data status).
